text: I've explored many cities, but I still get lost.
Have you ever asked a stranger for help?

Go straight ahead for two blocks.
Turn left at the traffic light.
The shop is next to the bank.
Walk past the square and it's on your right.
Excuse me, I'm a bit lost. Could you tell me how to get to the specialty coffee shop?
Of course. Go straight ahead for two blocks until you reach the big avenue. Then turn left at the traffic light.
Got it. So I cross at the crosswalk after the traffic light. Is the shop far from there?
No, it's close. Walk past the square and keep going. The shop is on the corner, next to a bookstore and opposite the old church.
Perfect. The old church is my landmark. And if I reach the roundabout?
Then you've gone too far, so just turn around. Don't worry, you can't miss it. Enjoy your coffee!
Excuse me, I'm looking for this place.
Could you tell me how to get there?
Go straight ahead and turn left at the corner.
It's on the corner, opposite the church.
Am I going the right way?

Hi Tuca, it's Mateo. You asked me how to reach the specialty coffee shop downtown, so here are the directions. From your hotel, turn right and walk straight ahead for two blocks until you reach the big avenue. At the second traffic light, cross at the crosswalk and keep walking on the same sidewalk. You will pass a small square on your left. The coffee shop is on the corner, right next to a bookstore and across from the old church. If you reach the roundabout, you have gone too far. The church is the easiest landmark, so just look for it. Honestly, I wish I could meet you there, but I'm stuck at work. Enjoy the coffee, and call me if you get lost.

Hey, it's Tuca. I finally found that famous coffee shop, and the walk was easier than I expected. I just followed Mateo's directions: I turned right, went straight ahead for two blocks, and crossed at the crosswalk by the big avenue. The little square was the perfect landmark. The shop sits right on the corner, next to a bookstore and opposite a beautiful old church. Yesterday I wasted a whole hour walking the wrong way, so next time I'll just ask a local right away. Anyway, the coffee here is incredible, very different from ours back at the farm. Come and visit, and I'll take you here myself.

Tuca leaves her hotel and realizes she does not know the way. She stops a friendly local and asks for directions. The local tells her to go straight ahead and turn at the traffic light. Tuca walks past the square and finds the corner. She arrives at the coffee shop, right next to the bookstore.
